results.rendered_html: anu-vākyā f. the verse to be recited by the Hotṛ or Maitrāvaruṇa priest, in which the god is invoked to partake of the offering intended for him,  ŚBr.

results.rendered_html:
anuvākyā (von vac mit anu) f. (sc. ṛc) der von dem Hotar zu recitirende [Page1-0220] Vers, in welchem die Gottheit von der ihr bestimmten Gabe in Kenntniss gesetzt und dazu eingeladen wird,  ŚAT. BR. 1,4,2,18 . 6,3,27 . 7,2,7 . 11 . 12 . u. s. w.
— Vgl. yājyā, puro'nuvākyā.

results.rendered_html: anuvākyā [vac-ṇyat-kutvaṃ] A verse recited by the Hotṛ priest, in which the god is invoked to accept the offering prepared for him, or a sort of priest;  Śi. 14. 20  ( praśāstṛpāṭhyā tadabhāve hotṛpāṭhyā devatāhvānī ṛk  Malli. ).
